behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure detection depends on handoff between file-loading, env-override merge, and validation/message formatting. Unit tests of validator or formatter alone can miss cross-module absence handling. No explicit OS/subprocess/external contract requirement and no AI dependency, so System/Agentic are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader with a temp config file missing `database.host` and with `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST` unset; assert the surfaced error message contains the exact path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This is an env-to-path-resolution interaction in the loader stack (env reader + path resolver + file locator). A single-function unit test can miss wiring issues. Higher levels are not required because behavior text does not require subprocess/CLI contract validation, only correct internal resolution.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory containing `tool/config.yaml`; invoke loader and assert it reads that file (and not `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`).

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is precedence across two local sources (YAML config + env overrides), i.e., a module interaction/handoff bug class. No explicit external contract, OS-specific behavior beyond normal env access, or AI/ML output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Create config with a value (e.g., `database.host: filehost`), set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=envhost`, load config, and assert effective value is `envhost`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Need real interaction with YAML parser exception metadata plus loader error translation. Unit-only formatting tests can hide parser-to-message wiring failures. System was considered but rejected: behavior text does not require validating CLI exit-code/stderr contract via subprocess, only presence of line number in the surfaced message.
behaviors.test_description: Provide malformed YAML in a temp config file, invoke loader, and assert the resulting error includes the parser-reported line number.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires proving externally visible filesystem side effects (directory creation + defaults file write) via round-trip observation, which triggers the SYSTEM TRIAD side-effect rule. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text explicitly requires observable creation/write at startup, not just internal function calls.
behaviors.test_description: Start with a clean temp HOME/XDG config root where config dir is absent; run startup path, then assert directory exists, defaults file exists with expected baseline content, and loading proceeds successfully afterward.
